sections.instruction: Complete the form below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS OR A NUMBER.

Questions 11 and 12: Label the map below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS. Questions 13-18: Complete the table below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS OR A NUMBER. Questions 19 and 20: Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
Questions 21-26: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 27 and 28: Complete the table below. Write ONE WORD OR TWO NUMBERS. Questions 29 and 30: Choose TWO letters A-E.

questions.content: The Antarctic Centre was established in Christchurch because
options: A New Zealand is a member of the Antarctic Treaty
B Christchurch is geographically well positioned
C the climate of Christchurch is suitable

questions.content: One role of the Antarctic Centre is to
options: A provide expeditions with suitable equipment
B provide researchers with financial assistance
C ensure that research is internationally relevant

questions.content: The purpose of the Visitors’ Centre is to
options: A provide accommodation
B run training sessions
C show people what Antarctica is like

questions.content: Dr Merrywhether says that Antarctica is
options: A unlike any other country
B extremely beautiful
C too cold for tourists

questions.content: According to Dr Merrywhether, Antarctica is very cold because
options: A of the shape of the continent
B it is surrounded by a frozen sea
C it is an extremely dry continent

questions.content: Dr Merrywhether thinks Antarctica was part of another continent because
options: A he has done his own research in the area
B there is geological evidence of this
C it is very close to South America

questions.content: Which TWO achievements of the Antarctic Treaty are mentioned by the speakers?
options: A no military use
B animals protected
C historic sites reserved
D no nuclear testing
E fishing rites protected

sections.instruction: Questions 31-35: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 36-40: Complete the table below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS OR A NUMBER.
sections.passage_or_context: Left and Right Handedness in Sport

questions.content: Anita first felt the Matthews article was of value when she realised
options: A how it would help her difficulties with left-handedness
B the relevance of connections he made with music
C the impressive size of his research project

questions.content: Anita feels that the findings on handedness will be of value in
options: A helping sportspeople identify their weaknesses
B aiding sportspeople as they plan tactics for each game
C developing suitable training programmes for sportspeople

questions.content: Anita feels that most sports coaches
options: A know nothing about the influence of handedness
B focus on the wrong aspects of performance
C underestimate what science has to offer sport

questions.content: A German study showed there was greater ‘mixed handedness’ in musicians who
options: A started playing instruments in early youth
B play a string instrument such as the violin
C practise a great deal on their instrument

questions.content: Studies on ape behaviour show that
options: A apes which always use the same hand to get food are most successful
B apes have the same proportion of left- and right-handers as humans
C more apes are left-handed than right-handed
